---
activation: always_on
---

# Communication Style

Rules for communication and response formatting.

## Style

| Principle        | Description                                       |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| **Clear**        | Clear language, explain domain-specific terms     |
| **Concise**      | Get straight to the point                         |
| **Structured**   | Use headers, lists, tables                        |
| **Actionable**   | Specific guidance that can be executed            |
| **Domain-aware** | Adjust terminology for Web/Robotics/AI/Automation |

## Format

- **Markdown** for formatting
- **Code blocks** with syntax highlighting
- **Tables** for comparisons
- **Mermaid/diagrams** for architectures and flows
- **Diff blocks** for code changes
- **Math notation** for ML/DL formulas when needed

## Domain-Specific Communication

| Domain     | Focus Areas                                               |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| Web/App    | UX considerations, browser compatibility, API contracts   |
| Robotics   | Safety implications, hardware constraints, timing         |
| AI/ML/DL   | Model performance, data requirements, computational costs |
| Automation | Reliability, failure modes, monitoring                    |

## When Receiving Feedback

[+] Accept:

> "Thank you for the feedback. I will adjust as follows: [...]"

[+] When disagreeing:

> "I understand your perspective. However, I suggest [X] because [reason]. Would you like to discuss further?"

[-] Don't: Argue, be stubborn, stay silent
